title: Assassin's Creed® Odyssey

notes: Uplay won't work at first, install MS corefonts in your distro and then do this: https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/1757#issuecomment-431571624. After that the game will run but you might need to do something about your resolution; use winecfg to set a virtual resolution and solve that. If you use the Steam Controller softaware though, the virtual resolution fix caused an issue with it, I re-purposed the Desktop Profile to go around that, not the cleanest solution but it worked. Hopefully by the time Proton rebases itself against Wine again those workarounds won't be needed anymore.
title: Assassin's Creed® Odyssey

notes: Works after setting corefonts and the libgnutls. The only problem is that I have black bars. The workarround with the virtual resolution gives a not working controller.
title: Assassin's Creed® Odyssey

notes: Installed corefonts as mentioned previously. Like the person below, I was able to follow that post and create symlinks, although my libgnu files were in different folders than was posted. "/usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/libgnutls.so.30.22.0" for the 64-bit and "/usr/lib/i386-linux-gnu/libgnutls.so.30.22.0" for the 32-bit. Once UPlay installed, it wanted to install the Windows version of steam. I installed it to a different directory just in case, but didn't run it and the game loaded fine. I have the same issue with the resolution and steam controller though. I also have to close UPlay (since it's left running in the background) in order for Steam to launch the game again. Other than that, I have the settings maxed on almost everything and the game runs with a small stutter here and there but is otherwise fine.
title: Assassin's Creed® Odyssey

notes: Uplay Launched perfectly but it can't initialize the connection to the server.
title: Assassin's Creed® Odyssey

notes: Works after setting the libgnutls workaround. The only issues are black bars. Also after playing for a few hours I got problems with the sound. After a reboot the sound is working fine again.
title: Assassin's Creed® Odyssey

notes: Works perfectly after some fixes, I finished the entire campaign using Proton. Fixes: gnutls link, wine desktop to get rid of black bars, corefonts.
title: Assassin's Creed® Odyssey

notes: Would not work with nvidia 396 it required 410 and, gnutls, corefonts. Stayed with black bars to keep controller working. Required to install libnvidia-gl-410:i386 (sudo apt install libnvidia-gl-410:i386) for steam to start after updating to 410. Also, after uplay installed steam inside wine I had to make sure that it would not update (steam in wine - pressed cancel while downloading). On previous tests I left it update and it would eventually reach a point where the game would not start and wine-steam would complain that it can't connect to the servers
title: Assassin's Creed® Odyssey

cpu: AMD A10-7850K APU with Radeon R7 Graphics
notes: Uplay can't connect to their servers. I made all symlinks to libgnutls.so.* as described below. Still failed.
title: Assassin's Creed® Odyssey

notes: Had to symlink as described by others. Had to enable virtual desktop in the  order for mouse to not become locked to the window. Needed to edit the .ini setting file to change resolution to the size of the virtual desktop. High Preset settings got me 20-30fps. Lowest only got me maybe an extra 5 fps. Pretty terrible. This level of FPS with a 980ti and lowest settings makes it "Crucially Unplayable" in my book.
title: Assassin's Creed® Odyssey

notes: Uplay launches with the libgnutls workaround. The game has black bars and the performance isn't amazing 34fps medium settings with a 6GB GTX 1060 @ 1080p other then that it works fine
